readings.title: A Study of Branch Prediction Strategies
readings.note: The foundational 1981 paper introducing 2-bit saturating counters for branch prediction. Establishes the theoretical framework for dynamic branch prediction that all subsequent work builds upon.

readings.title: Combining Branch Predictors (Technical Report TN-36)
readings.note: Introduces the gshare predictor and the tournament (hybrid) predictor. The gshare design -- XORing global history with PC to index a prediction table -- remains a foundational technique used in all modern predictors.

readings.title: A Case for (Partially) TAgged GEometric History Length Branch Prediction
readings.note: The original TAGE paper (2006). Introduces the idea of tagged predictor tables with geometrically increasing history lengths, which has dominated every Championship Branch Prediction competition since its introduction.

readings.title: Dynamic Branch Prediction with Perceptrons
readings.authors: Daniel A. Jimenez and Calvin Lin
readings.note: The HPCA 2001 paper that applies single-layer neural networks (perceptrons) to branch prediction. Key insight: linear scaling with history length versus exponential for table-based designs.

readings.title: Validity of the Single Processor Approach to Achieving Large Scale Computing Capabilities
readings.note: Amdahl's seminal 1967 paper establishing the fundamental limit on speedup from parallelism. Required reading for understanding why the serial fraction dominates performance scaling.

readings.title: Reevaluating Amdahl's Law
readings.note: The 1988 paper that complements Amdahl's Law with a scaled (weak scaling) perspective: if you scale the problem size with processor count, the serial fraction becomes less dominant.

readings.title: Branch Prediction Is Not A Solved Problem
readings.note: A 2019 study showing that even with state-of-the-art TAGE-SC-L predictors, modern server workloads still waste an average of 9.2% of cycles due to branch mispredictions. Motivates continued research.

readings.title: Computer Organization and Design: RISC-V Edition, Chapter 4 (The Processor)
readings.authors: David A. Patterson and John L. Hennessy
readings.note: The standard textbook treatment of pipelining, hazards, forwarding, and stalling. Chapter 4 covers the 5-stage RISC-V pipeline in detail with complete datapath diagrams.

readings.title: Zen 5's 2-Ahead Branch Predictor Unit: How a 30-Year-Old Idea Allows for New Tricks
readings.note: Detailed analysis of AMD Zen 5's innovative 2-ahead branch predictor that predicts two taken branches per cycle, doubling fetch throughput for branch-heavy code.
